title: AutoZone, Inc. (AZO) Q1 2023 Earnings Call Transcript
text: AutoZone, Inc. (NYSE:AZO ) Q1 2023 Results Conference Call December 6, 2022 10:00 AM ET Company Participants Brian Campbell - VP, IR and Tax/Treasurer Bill Rhodes - Chairman, President and CEO Jamere Jackson - EVP and CFO Conference Call Participants Brian Nagel - Oppenheimer Simeon Gutman - Morgan Stanley Kate McShane - Goldman Sachs Chris Horvers - JP Morgan Michael Lasser - UBS Daniel Imbro - Stephens David Bellinger - MKM Partners Mike Baker - D.A. Davidson Operator Good morning, ladies and gentlemen, and welcome to AutoZone's 2023 First Quarter Earnings Release Conference Call.

title: AutoZone Adds Tech and Stores to Meet Auto Parts Demand
text: AutoZone is growing its sales to both professional repair shops and do-it-yourselfers. The auto parts chain said in a Tuesday (Dec. 6) press release that its domestic same-store sales were up 5.6% during the quarter ended Nov. 19, on top of the 13.6% gain it made last year.

title: AutoZone Stock Skids After Earnings Beat As Inflation Boosts Inventory
text: AutoZone reported earnings and revenue growth to start fiscal year 2023, even as used car prices are beginning to come down. The post AutoZone Stock Skids After Earnings Beat As Inflation Boosts Inventory appeared first on Investor's Business Daily.

title: Autozone Shares 5.6% Jump in Same-Store Sales for Fiscal Q1
text: Autozone Inc (NYSE:AZO) stepped into the earnings confessional early this morning, posting s 5.6% jump in same-store sales, and a rise in earnings per share (EPS) to $27.45 -- topping expectations.

title: AutoZone (AZO) Beats Q1 Earnings and Revenue Estimates
text: AutoZone (AZO) delivered earnings and revenue surprises of 9.15% and 3.88%, respectively, for the quarter ended November 2022. Do the numbers hold clues to what lies ahead for the stock?

title: Is Autozone Ready To Rally For Another Profitable Lap?
text: Autozone and its competitors are being driven by robust aftermarket activity and they are returning capital to shareholders.

title: AutoZone tops expectations on revenue and earnings
text: AutoZone Inc. AZO, -1.74% reported better-than-expected revenue and earnings Tuesday, driven by growth in both commercial and retail sales. The seller of automotive replacement parts recorded fiscal first-quarter net income of $539.3 million, or $27.45 a share, compared with net income of $555.2 million, or $25.69 a share, in the year-earlier period.

title: AutoZone 1st Quarter Same Store Sales Increase 5.6%; EPS Increases to $27.45
text: MEMPHIS, Tenn., Dec. 06, 2022 (GLOBE NEWSWIRE) -- AutoZone, Inc. (NYSE: AZO) today reported net sales of $4.0 billion for its first quarter (12 weeks) ended November 19, 2022, an increase of 8.6% from the first quarter of fiscal 2022 (12 weeks). Domestic same store sales, or sales for stores open at least one year, increased 5.6% for the quarter.

title: AutoZone (AZO) Likely to Maintain its Earnings Beat Run in Q1
text: The Zacks Consensus Estimate for AutoZone's (AZO) fiscal Q1 earnings and revenues is pegged at $25.15 per share and $3.84 billion, respectively.
